div.h2: Analysis of Key Remapping on Node Addition/Removal

div.p: In Mod Hashing, the entire mapping is tied to N. A change in N shifts the mathematical "center" of the entire dataset. In Consistent Hashing, nodes and keys inhabit the same identifier space (0 to 2^160). Adding a node is like placing a new bucket on a circle; it only catches what was previously falling into the next bucket.

p.em: Statistically, (N)/(N+1) of keys must move when adding a node.

div.p: Result: ~80% Key Remapping Massive Cache Invalidation / DB Stampede

p.em: Only keys in the new node's specific arc move.

div.p: Result: ~20% Key Remapping Minimal Cache Misses / Graceful Scale-up

g.text: Keys map to next node CW
Only keys in Node E's arc move
